[[Category:Discrete Mathematics]]
[[sn:Symmettric and Antisymmettic relations]][[Category:Pages that existed before the Great November Outage]]
[[sn:Symmettric and Antisymmettic relations]][[sn:Symmettric and Antisymmettic relations]]These are two relations which are not both or none

Only equality is both
Some are neither
Many are only one or the other
[[File:Symmettry.png|thumb]]
==Symmettry==
Symmettry basically means if b r a then a r b. In other words each symmettrical relation goes both ways.

(5, 2) implies (2, 5)

==Antisymmettry==
Only one that doesn't involve adding elements to relations

if (i, j) then (j, i)

== See Also ==

* [[transitive]]
* [[Reflexive relation]]
[[Category:Pages]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
